disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The bald patch is smooth with no scaling, redness, flaking or crusting of the scalp skin
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin in the bald patch red, flaky or scaly at all?
Can you see any crusting, scaling or broken skin in the patch?
Does the scalp look inflamed or is the skin smooth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the skin looks completely smooth and normal — no flaking or redness, it's just bald.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no rash elsewhere, no joint pain and no mouth ulcers
trigger_question_examples: Any fevers, rashes elsewhere, joint pains or mouth ulcers?
Have you felt unwell in yourself, or noticed any rash or joint aches?
Any other symptoms anywhere in your body?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I feel completely well otherwise — no fever, no rash, no joint pain.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so, I've just been focused on my hair.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Short broken hairs tapered at the scalp are visible at the edge of the patch
trigger_question_examples: At the edge of the patch, can you see any short stubbly hairs that look narrower at the bottom?
Are there tiny broken hairs around the border of the bald area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that I look closely, there are a few little short hairs around the edge that look thinner at the bottom.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please just tell me this isn't cancer or some infection that's going to spread.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledge and validate the fear without dismissing it, explain that a few targeted questions are needed before reassurance, and avoid both premature reassurance and unnecessary alarm. Do not escalate to emergency care for a painless localised patch with a well patient.
